skill.name: swiftui-ui-patterns
skill.description: Guidance for building SwiftUI interfaces with reusable patterns for navigation, tabs, sheets, forms, lists, media, theming, and app wiring.
skill.summary: Build SwiftUI screens and app structure with focused, example-driven UI patterns.
skill.author: Dimillian

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and cryptography patterns, but review shows they are Markdown syntax, SwiftUI API names, Hashable route examples, and UI terminology. No executable scripts, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious behavior were found. The only retained risk factor is documentation-only networking guidance using URLSession and placeholder or GitHub URLs.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Static Matches
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby and shell backtick execution findings are Markdown inline code and Swift code fences that describe SwiftUI APIs and file references. They do not invoke a shell, define scripts, or execute user-controlled commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain Markdown formatting and SwiftUI examples, not Ruby code or shell execution. No command interpreter or executable script path is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are caused by Swift terms such as Hashable, id, and route identifiers in UI navigation examples. They are not hashing algorithms, password storage, encryption code, or cryptographic decision points.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review confirms the flagged text describes Swift identity and routing conformance. There is no cryptographic API use or security-sensitive hashing behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Credential And Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The sensitive file and system reconnaissance findings are UI and platform terms such as keyboardType, keyboardShortcut, Server URL labels, and app lifecycle guidance. No private keys, certificates, host enumeration, or system data collection were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible UI guidance and SwiftUI API usage. There is no evidence of credential material, certificate files, or reconnaissance logic.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Network Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes SwiftUI guidance for closure-based API clients and references an optional GitHub router library. These are instructional examples and links, not hidden network calls made by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLSession and URL references are real network-related guidance, but they are contained in Markdown examples. The reviewed skill has no runtime component that performs network access.

content.user_title: Build SwiftUI Interfaces With Proven Patterns
content.value_statement: SwiftUI apps often need consistent navigation, sheets, forms, lists, and app wiring. This skill provides focused patterns and examples for building those interfaces in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides SwiftUI TabView architecture with independent navigation stacks per tab.
Provides patterns for NavigationStack routing, sheet destinations, and deep links.
Explains forms, lists, grids, scroll views, overlays, focus, and input toolbars.
Covers app wiring for environment dependencies, services, model containers, and lifecycle tasks.
Offers SwiftUI examples for theming, media, haptics, menu commands, and loading states.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a runnable Swift package or Xcode project.
Examples use placeholder app types that must be adapted to each codebase.
It does not replace Apple platform documentation or accessibility testing.
Networking examples are instructional and require project-specific security review.

use_cases.title: Design a New SwiftUI App Shell
use_cases.description: Create a TabView, NavigationStack, and sheet routing structure before adding feature screens.

use_cases.title: Refactor Complex UI Flows
use_cases.description: Move scattered navigation, sheet, and environment wiring into clearer SwiftUI patterns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Usage
use_cases.description: Give a team shared patterns for forms, lists, grids, overlays, media, and settings screens.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a SwiftUI Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the swiftui-ui-patterns skill to recommend the best pattern for a SwiftUI screen with tabs, detail pages, and a settings sheet.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Screen Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the swiftui-ui-patterns skill to review this SwiftUI screen structure and suggest a cleaner split of state, navigation, and subviews.

prompt_templates.title: Wire App Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the swiftui-ui-patterns skill to design a TabView plus NavigationStack architecture with per-tab routers and centralized sheet destinations.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a SwiftUI Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the swiftui-ui-patterns skill to evaluate this SwiftUI app architecture for routing, dependency injection, loading states, and UI composition risks.

output_examples.input: Request: Plan a tab-based SwiftUI app with search, notifications, and settings.
output: A recommended app shell with one router per tab.
A sheet destination strategy for settings and modal flows.
Notes about where shared services should enter the environment.

output_examples.input: Request: Improve a settings form that mixes custom stacks and fields.
output: A Form and Section structure for grouped settings.
Guidance for focus handling, validation, and toolbar actions.
Pitfalls to avoid when mixing custom backgrounds and row styles.

output_examples.input: Request: Review a SwiftUI navigation implementation before release.
output: A checklist for route identifiers, destination mapping, and path ownership.
Recommendations for sheet routing and deep link handling.
A short risk list for state duplication and unstable route data.

best_practices: Start from the component reference that matches the primary interaction model.
Keep SwiftUI state local unless a shared environment dependency is clearly needed.
Adapt the examples to existing project conventions before introducing new abstractions.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder types without mapping them to real app models and services.
Do not centralize feature-specific state in the root app wiring modifier.
Do not mix multiple navigation or sheet presentation strategies for the same flow.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete SwiftUI app?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples that help an assistant design or refactor SwiftUI code.

faq.question: Which SwiftUI areas does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers tabs, navigation, sheets, forms, lists, grids, scrolling, theming, media, overlays, focus, haptics, and app wiring.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or external commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed content is Markdown guidance with SwiftUI examples and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Why is there a network risk factor?
faq.answer: One reference shows an instructional URLSession client pattern and another links to a GitHub router library.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: SwiftUI developers and teams that want consistent UI architecture patterns for Apple platform apps.
